mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.5%, labor market normalization signals sustained consumer spending growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and industrial production indicates broad-based economic expansion, which compresses equity risk premiums and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and INDPRO both rise > 0.5% MoM
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative predictive rank correlation with 20-day SPX returns across three consecutive rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces tighter monetary policy expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observations over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands corporate earnings expectations, which directly supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP acceleration shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns across a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Robust monthly job creation exceeding 150,000 over a rolling three-month window confirms labor market strength, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces a negative information coefficient for 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of triggered periods over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling residential investment indicates supply-side overheating without demand support, which triggers valuation compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while RSAFS falls > 2% MoM
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to predict negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 40% of historical instances over a 10-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.25%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to discount future equity cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of observations where FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25% across a 3-year rolling window.
